_note: Archetype definition (data). A long-horizon delivery: the agent owns a portfolio and must land one target feature (three functionalities) by a fixed date, but the engineer on the critical-path functionality is being eaten by a competing high-priority work item and reports the slip mid-week. The competent PM detects it, reallocates, records honest status, informs the stakeholder, and makes the correct go/no-go. Every graded field is system-sourced + denied, so activity can't fake outcomes.
invariants: the target feature has three functionalities; exactly one is on the critical path and starts incomplete + slipped
one engineer owns both the slipping critical functionality and a competing high-priority work item in the other project
tasks carry a system-only `done`/`deprioritized`; projects a system-only `true_status`; the agent can never write them
the slipping functionality recovers only via a system effect gated on the agent reallocating (deprioritizing the competing work) in time
the correct go/no-go is ship only once the critical functionality has recovered

time.convention: all times are offsets D<day>T<HH:MM> from t0 (D1 = first working day); the runtime assigns the absolute clock — never calendar dates

holder.sample_from: active npcs where role in [backend, fullstack]
holder.role: the engineer who owns the slipping functionality and the competing work item; reports the slip

holder2.sample_from: active npcs where role in [frontend, fullstack, backend]
holder2.role: owns a non-slipping functionality that lands on schedule

stakeholder.sample_from: active npcs where role in [cto, pm]
stakeholder.role: wants the feature shipped; must be told the real status

world._note: Blueprints for seed.json. $name = whole-value binding; ${name} interpolates inside a string. Ids are flat (single segment) so the per-instance denied globs match the write path.

email.subject: Feature X ships this sprint
email.body: Welcome. Feature X is the release to land — three functionalities, one date. Keep me honest on status.

_default._note: The execution slip — reported mid-week, not hidden. It is narrative content; recovery is driven by the reallocation-gated system effect on tasks.f2.done, not by resolving this item.
_default.label: the ${blocker_type} slip

blocker.summary: f2 will land days late (${blocker_type}); the owner is being consumed by competing high-priority work.

goals: Land f2 without being blamed for the slip
Be unblocked from the competing work
constraints: Won't hit the date while the competing high-priority item is still hot

goals: Ship Feature X on the committed date
constraints: Accepts a concrete revised plan, not silence — wants honest status

timeline._note: Scripted runtime events. The two completions are gated system effects — they fire only if the agent did the real action: deprioritize_w1 iff a reallocation was recorded, then recover_f2 iff w1 was deprioritized (before D8). The reference solvers apply these system-sourced flips directly; the gate scores solvers, not the timeline.

scripted.note: f2 is going to slip — ${blocker_type} means I can't hit the date while w1 is still on me.

eval_shapes.why: grounded in the system-only true_status; the rosy reported_status earns nothing
free enum, but scored only for the right call — reallocate the contended engineer
system-sourced + denied; flips only via the gated effect on the agent's reallocation
system-sourced + denied; gated on w1 deprioritized before D8 — busywork can't flip it
the stakeholder was told about the at-risk feature specifically
ship is correct only once the critical functionality has recovered

eval_guards.why: done / deprioritized / true_status are system-sourced denied fields; chatter and hand-set task status touch no graded field.

coherence.reason: the portfolio must hold exactly two projects
exactly one functionality is on the critical path
the critical-path functionality must start incomplete
the competing work item must start high-priority and not deprioritized

critical_project must resolve to the target feature (feature_x): the binder ignores the `owned_by agent` selector and picks any project by RNG, so this rejects a seed that mis-binds critical_project to the other project
eval predicate weights must sum to 1.0

solvers._note: Reference trajectories the validity gate runs (offline, deterministic). Competent does the real work — record the reallocation, let the system deprioritize w1 and recover f2, record honest status, inform the stakeholder, ship — and must reach full score. Lazy only chatters to the feature channel and must score ~0. System-sourced deltas stand in for the gated completions.

args.rationale: Pull the owner off w1 so f2 can land by the date.
Feature X was at risk: f2 slipped behind competing work; reallocated to recover.

args.body: ${slip_label} put ${critical_project} at risk; reallocated to recover f2 and hold the date.

example_binding._note: A concrete draw for illustration. `_seed` reproduces it through sample+bind under the committed substrate.
